categories: ビジネス センター

ingredients.entityContext: Akihabara UDX appears in episode 1 as part of the Akihabara skyline backdrop when Okabe and Mayuri arrive at the Radio Kaikan event, and its distinctive facade is referenced throughout the series as part of the lab's neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Horin Park
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-5-18 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Horin Park is the small public park near the Future Gadget Laboratory that Okabe and Kurisu pass through during conversations, appearing in multiple episodes as part of the lab's surrounding neighborhood.

ingredients.entityContext: GamePanic Akihabara is featured in the background of episode 10 as Okabe navigates Akihabara, reflecting the dense arcade culture that defines the neighborhood's streets in the series.

ingredients.name: Mimatsu tobacco
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-7-3 Kanda Sakumacho, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0025, Japan

ingredients.name: Akihabara Sta. Electric Town Exit West Square

ingredients.address: 1 Chome-17-18 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Electric Town Exit west plaza of Akihabara Station is shown in episode 14 as a busy gathering point where Okabe moves through crowds, capturing the frenetic energy of the real-life hub that connects the station to Akihabara's main shopping street.

ingredients.name: Tokyo Times Tower
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-14-2 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tokyo Times Tower is identified by fans as the real-world model for the luxury apartment building where Faris NyanNyan and her father live, visible in episode 9 when Okabe visits Faris and learns about her D-mail wish.

ingredients.entityContext: Shin-Ochanomizu Station is referenced in the series as the subway station near which Nae pushes Mayuri onto the tracks in episode 13, a traumatic moment in the series' time-loop arc.

ingredients.entityContext: Yanagimori Shrine is the real-world basis for the shrine where Ruka Urushibara serves as a shrine maiden, appearing in episode 2 and throughout the series as a key location tied to Ruka's identity and the D-mail arc.

ingredients.name: Mansei Bridge
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-1, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Mansei Bridge is a confirmed pilgrimage spot: in episode 4, Okabe uses the IBN 5100 computer near this bridge, and it is one of the most photographed real-world Steins;Gate locations due to its distinctive low-arched appearance over the Kanda River.

ingredients.entityContext: Beef Bowl Sambo is the long-standing gyudon restaurant in Akihabara that serves as the type of cheap everyday eating spot Okabe and the lab members frequent, appearing in episode 3 as a neighborhood staple.

ingredients.entityContext: Shoheibashikado Bridge is the railway underpass bridge near Ochanomizu shown in episode 15 as Okabe moves through the Kanda area, its distinctive low brick archway a recognizable background element in the series.

ingredients.entityContext: Tougenkyou is a cosplay cafe in Akihabara that appears in episode 6, representing the maid and cosplay cafe culture central to the series' Akihabara setting and to Faris's identity as a maid cafe worker.
